user_query:
I want a simple ASCII cheat sheet for the I–V–vi–IV progression in G major. Use the chord names: g d em c (lowercase given on purpose). Please:
- Normalize the chord names to the correct case expected by chord-diagram tools (e.g., "em" -> "Em").
- Generate a side-by-side ASCII chord diagram for the sequence G D Em C and save it to output/progression.txt.
- Also generate individual chord diagram files at:
  - output/diagrams/G.txt
  - output/diagrams/D.txt
  - output/diagrams/Em.txt
  - output/diagrams/C.txt
- Write output/normalized_chords.json containing the final ordered list of normalized chord names as a JSON array ["G","D","Em","C"].
- If any chord is unsupported, list them in output/unsupported.json as a JSON array; otherwise write an empty array [].
Make sure the diagrams are monospace-friendly and preserve the layout exactly in the text files.
